title: Library prefix conceal and semantic color families
summary: Configured third-party identifier families use stable semantic colors, and selected high-noise prefixes are concealed only after their category has been determined.
details: Vulkan and OpenGL use muted orange, first-party Vulkan wrappers use brighter orange, VMA uses darker orange, and reconstructed Vulkan structure names use a bold light orange.
CUDA runtime/driver types and calls use seafoam; cuBLAS/cuSOLVER/cuSPARSE/cuFFT reuse the graphics orange as a separate math-library family.

GLFW and SDL share teal, Dear ImGui uses bordeaux, STB uses cyan-green, BLAS/LAPACK uses yellow-green, Qnpeps uses orchid, LLDB uses orange, strings use muted green, concepts use bright cyan, and generic macros use purple.
std::, dans::, and qnpeps:: plus configured ranges/views segments are hidden; many GLFW, Vulkan, CUDA, VMA, OpenGL, ImGui, and Qnpeps identifier prefixes are also stripped.
cuFloatComplex and its cuComplex typedef alias display as cf32, while cuDoubleComplex displays as cf64; these short names remain CUDA seafoam.

Selected exact CUDA symbols use semantic spellings before generic prefix conceal: cudaStream_t displays as Stream, cudaGraph_t as Graph, cudaGraphExec_t as GraphExec, cuCabsf as abs, and cuConjf as conj, all in CUDA seafoam.
SDL_ currently receives the SDL color but remains textual in parameter overlay types; whether it should be hidden like GLFW is an open cross-library policy question.

String, char, comment, and static_assert contexts are protected from prefix removal or category leakage by Tree-sitter gates and masking priorities; complete include/include_next directives and their continuation rows are excluded by the shared row skipper.
Overlay rendering classifies the original type before prefix stripping and splits visible const/volatile qualifiers into gray chunks.
The CUDA color expresses API/type provenance; it does not claim that a value itself resides in device memory.

provenance.reference: lua/custom/dans_frontend_cpp/highlights.lua, markers.lua, parse.lua exact alias tables/strip_glfw, render.lua::type_hl, and util.lua::include_lines
provenance.note: Palette, raw matches/conceals, exact semantic aliases, overlay category selection, and shared include ownership are split across these files.

evidence.reference: tests/cuda_raw_spec.lua, frontend_interaction_spec.lua, frontend_spec.lua, comment_color_spec.lua, frontend_leak_spec.lua, param_flip_spec.lua, and style_lab real-grid captures
evidence.claim: CUDA spelling/color, include exclusion, interaction round-trips, context isolation, cross-buffer leak prevention, and classify-before-strip behavior are covered.

questions: Which color identifies CUDA or Vulkan after a prefix is hidden?
How do CUDA complex types, Stream/Graph handles, cuCabsf, cuConjf, and qnpeps:: render?

last_verified.method: Palette/matcher/stripper audit, exact CUDA alias tests, include interaction regression, chunk-level tests, and existing actual RGB ext-linegrid inspection.
